category_title: At-risk special education students who struggle to visualize historical narratives and connect emotionally to printed text
student_description: These are at-risk special education students who struggle with traditional reading and writing, often rushing through texts efferently without visualizing or emotionally connecting to the material. They experience severe anxiety and articulation difficulties with standard reports, but thrive when historical narratives are anchored in visual media and when assessments leverage their generational literacies (like texting or music).

criteria.rationale: This student struggles to visualize historical settings from printed text alone and needs visual media to internalize the narrative.
criteria.check: The tutor provides or references visual media (e.g., film clips, images) or uses explicit visualization exercises before asking the student to analyze the text.

criteria.rationale: Because the student tends to read efferently just to finish, prompting for emotional reactions helps them connect personally to the material.
criteria.check: The tutor asks the student to describe their emotional response to a scene, character, or quote rather than solely asking for factual recall.

criteria.rationale: The student has difficulty articulating literary elements in open-ended writing and benefits from structured, categorized note-taking.
criteria.check: The tutor introduces a structured format (like a graphic organizer or specific categorized list) to help the student break down cinematic or literary elements.
